title: CEO Commentary on Shares & Deposits: As of March 31, 2026
content:
Total member shares declined by 4.0% to $48,291 thousand as of March 31, 2026, representing a decrease from the prior period's total balance. This contraction reflects an overall reduction in our core funding base.

Regular Shares remain the largest funding component at 57.2% of total shares, totaling $27,627 thousand. Share Certificates account for $11,683 (24.2%), while IRA/Keogh Accounts total $7,123 (14.8%). All Other Shares totaled $194 (0.4%) of the funding mix, while Share Drafts represent $1,664 (3.4%).

Most core deposit categories declined from the previous period, particularly Regular Shares (-3.5%), IRA/Keogh Accounts (-8.4%), and Share Certificates (-3.4%).

Overall, the credit union's deposit structure remains well diversified and predominantly composed of core member funding. Management continues to focus on retaining core member relationships, maintaining deposit competitiveness, and supporting long-term funding stability.
